title: The lark's head knot
subtitle: Mounting cord onto a dowel, ring, or branch to begin a macramé piece
excerpt: The lark's head knot is how most macramé work begins: it folds a length of cord in half and attaches both ends to a mounting bar in a single pass. The reverse lark's head produces the same knot but with the bar on the back rather than the front, giving a different texture to the mounting row.
sourceNotes: The lark's head knot is a standard knot in macramé and knotted textile work. Its structure is equivalent to the girth hitch in outdoor rope work. Observation-led.

glossaryTerms.definition: A mounting knot formed by folding a cord in half and looping the folded end over a bar, then pulling both working ends through the loop. It attaches two working cords to the bar in one step.
The rod, dowel, ring, or branch to which macramé cords are attached at the start of a piece. The mounting bar determines the width and hanging point of the work.
The cord that forms the knots. In macramé, some cords are knotting (working) cords and others are core (carrier) cords around which knots are tied.

content.text: so that both halves of the cord hang below as

. A 2 m cord folded and mounted with a lark's head knot gives two working cords each 1 m long (before accounting for knot consumption). Most macramé patterns specify cord lengths before mounting; follow the pattern instruction on how long to cut each piece.

steps: Fold the cord in half to find the midpoint.
Hold the fold and push the looped end up and over the front of the mounting bar.
Pull the loop down behind the bar so that it comes forward between the bar and the two hanging cord ends.
Pull both cord ends down firmly through the loop.
Tighten: pull both cord ends downward evenly so the knot seats against the bar.

content.text: The reverse lark's head produces the same attachment but with the bar at the back rather than the front of the knot, creating a different surface texture on the mounting row. It is often used when the top of the piece will be visible.

steps: Push the looped end down and under the back of the mounting bar.
Bring the loop up and over the bar toward you.
Pull both cord ends up through the loop (they will be above the bar momentarily).
Pull both cord ends back down and tighten so the knot sits with the bar on the back face.

content.text: Pin the mounting bar to the macramé board at a comfortable working height. Mount cords one at a time, working from left to right. After each knot, slide it against the previous one so the row is dense and even. Avoid gaps between knot heads, which weaken the appearance of the mounting row.

attrs.heading: Common problems
attrs.intro: One problem in lark's head mounting.

items.symptom: Knots slide along the bar and will not stay evenly spaced
items.cause: The bar is very smooth and polished (metal dowel, varnished wood) or the cord is slippery.
items.fix: Use T-pins to temporarily pin each knot in position as it is tied. Once several rows of knotting below the bar are complete, the tension locks the mounting knots in place.
